title: knautia kitaibelii subsp. tomentella
intro: Knautia kitaibelii subsp. tomentella, also known as the woolly or hairy knautia, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caprifoliaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, wounds, and skin irritations. The plant has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. Knautia kitaibelii subsp. tomentella can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as dried and used as a powder for various medicinal applications.
